title: School-Based Antecedent Interventions Derived from Structural Analyses: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
data:

outcomes. Antecedent interventions are a group of behavior management strategies

title: Beyond Code Reasoning: A Specification-Anchored Audit Framework for Expert-Augmented Security Verification
data:
M Kamba, H Murakami, A Sannai- arXiv preprint arXiv:2604.26495, 2026
Security-critical software is routinely audited by tools that reason about vulnerabilities 
as repository-local code patterns. Yet specification-governed systems--protocol 
stacks, consensus implementations, cryptographic libraries--are constrained by 
invariants and correctness conditions defined in natural-language specifications. 
When a vulnerability arises from what the specification requires rather than how code 
is written, code-level approaches lack the representational vocabulary to detect it

title: From Correctness to Code Quality: Formalizing Software Engineering Metrics for Evaluating General LLMs
data:
J Umre, AS Parihar, A Gupta- Proceedings of the 19th Innovations in Software, 2026
Despite the growing use of large language models (LLMs) for code generation, the 
majority of evaluations only consider functional correctness. We present a multi-
metric empirical study introducing five mathematically defined Software Engineering 
(SE) quality metrics, which are Error Handling Score (EHS), Input Validation Score 
(IVS), Maintainability Score (MS), Style & Structure (S3), and Documentation Score 
(DS). Each metric is normalized to [0, 1] with transparent feature definitions and
